item.title: Chapel Footer
item.dc:creator: nativechurch
item.content:encoded:
Explore resources related to your chapel
Native Community Chapel

We believe that as we allow the Holy Spirit to reveal His purposes and to presence Himself among His Church, people’s lives will be transformed.

For Donors

Your generosity supports our efforts to improve well being of our community. Native Chapel accepts your donation on the same note.

Donate
Newsletter Signup

Enter your email address to subscribe to the Native Chapel Newsletter. We respect your privacy and won’t share your information with anyone.

©Native Community Chapel, 2023. All Rights Reserved.

Facebook Twitter Youtube

item.title: About us page
item.dc:creator: nativechurch
item.content:encoded:
Message from Josephine Williams

We lead people to become disciples of Jesus Christ

Morbi porta malesuada ligula eget efficitur. Etiam non velit nec lacus tempor tempor. Duis pretium, massa non porttitor mollis, ante lacus vestibulum magna, in bibendum odio orci non leo. Aenean aliquam pretium ipsum non bibendum ante lacus vestibulum magna. Duis pretium, massa non porttitor mollis, ante lacus vestibulum magna, in bibendum odio orci non leo. Aenean aliquam pretium ipsum non bibendum ante lacus vestibulum magna.

Aliquam hendrerit porttitor ex vitae feugiat. Integer placerat augue sit amet nibh porttitor blandit. Morbi porta malesuada ligula eget efficitur. Etiam non velit nec lacus tempor tempor. Duis pretium, massa non porttitor mollis, ante lacus vestibulum magna, in bibendum odio orci non leo. Aenean aliquam pretium ipsum non bibendum ante lacus vestibulum magna.

Our Beliefs

The Trinity

Scripture

Salvation

Atonement

Christian Life

The Chapel

Baptism

Marriage

Our Story

Native Chapel membership is a commitment to one another—a willing pledge to guard, care, submit, support, and minister together for the building up of our church body.

Our Values

We value the empowering presence of the Holy Spirit in our midst, marking us as God’s people and manifesting Himself miraculously.

item.title: Home For Export
item.dc:creator: nativechurch
item.content:encoded:
Your Community.
Your Chapel.
Your People.

Check out COVID guidelines before you visit the Chapel. See details

About Chapel

We believe that as we allow the Holy Spirit to reveal His purposes
and to presence Himself among His Church, people’s lives will be transformed.
Our leadership is Spirit-led and our activities and programs are Spirit directed.

Worship

Join on Sundays at 09:15 & 10:45 in the morning and on demand.

Watch Live Streaming

Grow

Connect with others in the community and grow in faith & worship.

Your Community

Connect

Need prayer, counselling, or have questions? We want to help.

Get Help

Belong , Believe  and Transform

This Weekend

The Holy Spirit’s presence assures us of our relationship with Christ. He guides believers into all truth and exalts Christ. He convicts people of their sin, God’s righteousness, and the coming judgment. He comforts us, gives us spiritual gifts, and makes us more like Christ.

Learn More Next Thing 06 September Upcoming Event

Morning of Praise

Music Ministry days hr min sec [eventer_list month_filter="345" series="" count="2"]

Sermons

  • Soul Detox

    Watch Series

  • Jesus - Changes Everything

    Watch Series

  • His & Hers

    Watch Series

  • Pneuma - Life in the spirit

    Watch Series
Native Community Chapel

Podcast

Listen to our podcasts available for everyone including the youth & kids. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Nulla convallis egestas rhoncus.

Subscribe Now

Activities changing lives

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Nulla convallis egestas rhoncus. Donec facilisis fermentum sem, ac viverra ante luctus vel. Donec vel mauris quam.

Start your journey

Join your
community

Start Here

Chapel Education

Social responsibility becomes an aspect not of Christian mission only, but also of Christian conversion. It is impossible to be truly converted to God without being thereby converted to our neighbour.

Learn More

item.title: Who we are export
item.dc:creator: nativechurch
item.content:encoded:
Message from Josephine Williams

We lead people to become disciples of Jesus Christ

Morbi porta malesuada ligula eget efficitur. Etiam non velit nec lacus tempor tempor. Duis pretium, massa non porttitor mollis, ante lacus vestibulum magna, in bibendum odio orci non leo. Aenean aliquam pretium ipsum non bibendum ante lacus vestibulum magna. Duis pretium, massa non porttitor mollis, ante lacus vestibulum magna, in bibendum odio orci non leo. Aenean aliquam pretium ipsum non bibendum ante lacus vestibulum magna.

Aliquam hendrerit porttitor ex vitae feugiat. Integer placerat augue sit amet nibh porttitor blandit. Morbi porta malesuada ligula eget efficitur. Etiam non velit nec lacus tempor tempor. Duis pretium, massa non porttitor mollis, ante lacus vestibulum magna, in bibendum odio orci non leo. Aenean aliquam pretium ipsum non bibendum ante lacus vestibulum magna.

Our Beliefs

The Trinity

Scripture

Salvation

Atonement

Christian Life

The Chapel

Baptism

Marriage

Our Story

Native Chapel membership is a commitment to one another—a willing pledge to guard, care, submit, support, and minister together for the building up of our church body.

Our Values

We value the empowering presence of the Holy Spirit in our midst, marking us as God’s people and manifesting Himself miraculously.

item.title: baptism export
item.dc:creator: nativechurch
item.content:encoded:
Baptism is the beginning of our faith and our gateway into the Church.

It is the first of three Sacraments of Initiation. Through Baptism we are freed from sin and reborn as sons of God; we become members of Christ, are incorporated into the Church and made sharers in her mission: "Baptism is the sacrament of regeneration through water in the word".

Related Events

[eventer_grid layout="featured" ids="2423" month_filter="345" series="" column="2" count="1"]

How it works

How do I have my child baptized?

“But I must explain to you how all this mistaken idea of denouncing pleasure and praising pain was born and I will give you a complete account of the system, and expound the actual teachings of the great explorer of the truth, the master-builder of human happiness. No one rejects, dislikes, or avoids pleasure itself, because it is pleasure, but because those who do not know how to pursue pleasure rationally encounter consequences that are extremely painful. Nor again is there anyone who loves or pursues or desires to obtain pain of itself, because it is pain, but because occasionally circumstances occur in which toil and pain can procure him some great pleasure. To take a trivial example, which of us ever undertakes laborious physical exercise, except to obtain some advantage from it? But who has any right to find fault with a man who chooses to enjoy a pleasure that has no annoying consequences, or one who avoids a pain that produces no resultant pleasure?”

Section 1.10.33 of “de Finibus Bonorum et Malorum”, written by Cicero in 45 BC

“At vero eos et accusamus et iusto odio dignissimos ducimus qui blanditiis praesentium voluptatum deleniti atque corrupti quos dolores et quas molestias excepturi sint occaecati cupiditate non provident, similique sunt in culpa qui officia deserunt mollitia animi, id est laborum et dolorum fuga. Et harum quidem rerum facilis est et expedita distinctio. Nam libero tempore, cum soluta nobis est eligendi optio cumque nihil impedit quo minus id quod maxime placeat facere possimus, omnis voluptas assumenda est, omnis dolor repellendus. Temporibus autem quibusdam et aut officiis debitis aut rerum necessitatibus saepe eveniet ut et voluptates repudiandae sint et molestiae non recusandae. Itaque earum rerum hic tenetur a sapiente delectus, ut aut reiciendis voluptatibus maiores alias consequatur aut perferendis doloribus asperiores repellat.”

Baptism Process

Find some water

A person may be baptized anywhere – a river, pool, bathtub, hot tub, lake or the ocean. The Bible does not limit where someone must be baptized because the power of God’s forgiveness is in Jesus and not the water.

Go into the water

If the water source is large enough (like a pool, river, or baptistry), then you may go into the water with the one being baptized. If it is too small (like a bathtub), then just kneel next to the person as he/she sits down into the water.

Take their confession of faith

Ask, “Do you believe that Jesus is the Son of God? And do you trust Jesus as your Savior and commit to obey Jesus as your Lord?”

What to say

Say, “I now baptize you in the name of the Father, Son and Holy Spirit.” (This is what Jesus taught us to say in Matthew 28:19)

Lay the person down into the water and back up

Full immersion best represents dying to our old selves and being reborn in Jesus, but don’t be alarmed if someone’s elbow or toe pops up during the baptism. Jesus does the saving – not the water. Bonus point: Have the person hold his/her nose.

And do you trust Jesus as your Savior and commit to obey Jesus as your Lord?”","header_size":"div","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Rubik","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":14,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.5,"sizes":[]},"__globals__":{"typography_typography":""}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"2cfbd940","elType":"widget","settings":{"text":"Divider","color":"#6F717366","__globals__":{"color":""}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"divider"},{"id":"7755e45b","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"What to say","header_size":"h4","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":14,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"3e146078","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Say, “I now baptize you in the name of the Father, Son and Holy Spirit.” (This is what Jesus taught us to say in Matthew 28:19)","header_size":"div","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Rubik","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":14,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.5,"sizes":[]},"__globals__":{"typography_typography":""}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"6332139e","elType":"widget","settings":{"text":"Divider","color":"#6F717366","__globals__":{"color":""}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"divider"},{"id":"320c8ef1","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Lay the person down into the water and back up","header_size":"h4","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":14,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"2ab0a845","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Full immersion best represents dying to our old selves and being reborn in Jesus, but don’t be alarmed if someone’s elbow or toe pops up during the baptism. Jesus does the saving – not the water. Bonus point: Have the person hold his/her nose.","header_size":"div","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Rubik","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":14,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.5,"sizes":[]},"__globals__":{"typography_typography":""}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"}],"isInner":false}],"isInner":false}]

item.title: Home
item.dc:creator: nativechurch
item.content:encoded:
Your Community.
Your Chapel.
Your People.

Check out COVID guidelines before you visit the Chapel. See details

About Chapel

We believe that as we allow the Holy Spirit to reveal His purposes
and to presence Himself among His Church, people’s lives will be transformed.
Our leadership is Spirit-led and our activities and programs are Spirit directed.

Worship

Join on Sundays at 09:15 & 10:45 in the morning and on demand.

Watch Live Streaming

Grow

Connect with others in the community and grow in faith & worship.

Your Community

Connect

Need prayer, counselling, or have questions? We want to help.

Get Help

Belong , Believe  and Transform

This Weekend

The Holy Spirit’s presence assures us of our relationship with Christ. He guides believers into all truth and exalts Christ. He convicts people of their sin, God’s righteousness, and the coming judgment. He comforts us, gives us spiritual gifts, and makes us more like Christ.

Learn More Next Thing 06 January Upcoming Event

Morning of Praise

Music Ministry days hr min sec [eventer_list month_filter="345" series="" count="2"]

Sermons

  • Soul Detox

    Watch Series

  • Jesus - Changes Everything

    Watch Series

  • His & Hers

    Watch Series

  • Pneuma - Life in the spirit

    Watch Series
Native Community Chapel

Podcast

Listen to our podcasts available for everyone including the youth & kids. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Nulla convallis egestas rhoncus.

Subscribe Now

Activities changing lives

item.title: Know Jesus
item.dc:creator: nativechurch
item.content:encoded:
When you learn how to know Jesus, you begin an amazing journey into God's plan for your life.

“You are the light of the world. A city set on a hill cannot be hidden.”
Matthew 5:14

“Arise, shine, for your light has come, and the glory of the Lord has risen upon you.”
Isaiah 60:1

Download our
Free E-Book

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Nulla convallis egestas rhoncus. Donec facilisis fermentum sem, ac viverra ante luctus vel. Donec vel mauris quam.

Do not worry, we won't spam your email inbox.

Jesus Christ is the very representation and likeness of God. He was sent to show us the beauty and majesty of the King. He did not make His debut on that first Christmas morning in Bethlehem because He existed before creation “in the beginning.” In fact, it was “through Him” that “all things were made.” (John 1:3) Even when we look back to the book of Genesis, we read that God said, “Let us make man in our image” (Genesis 1:26) referencing the presence of Christ.

“In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God …. And the Word became flesh, and dwelt among us …. No one has seen God at any time; the only begotten God who is in the bosom of the Father, He has explained Him.”

John 1:1, 14, 18
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oh1Y0Xvs2Zs

How to know Jesus

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kvcVbMcRUkk

Does Jesus Know You?

item.title: Baptism Export
item.dc:creator: nativechurch
item.content:encoded:
Baptism is the beginning of our faith and our gateway into the Church.

It is the first of three Sacraments of Initiation. Through Baptism we are freed from sin and reborn as sons of God; we become members of Christ, are incorporated into the Church and made sharers in her mission: "Baptism is the sacrament of regeneration through water in the word".

Related Events

[eventer_grid layout="featured" ids="2423" month_filter="345" series="" column="2" count="1"]

How it works

How do I have my child baptized?

“But I must explain to you how all this mistaken idea of denouncing pleasure and praising pain was born and I will give you a complete account of the system, and expound the actual teachings of the great explorer of the truth, the master-builder of human happiness. No one rejects, dislikes, or avoids pleasure itself, because it is pleasure, but because those who do not know how to pursue pleasure rationally encounter consequences that are extremely painful. Nor again is there anyone who loves or pursues or desires to obtain pain of itself, because it is pain, but because occasionally circumstances occur in which toil and pain can procure him some great pleasure. To take a trivial example, which of us ever undertakes laborious physical exercise, except to obtain some advantage from it? But who has any right to find fault with a man who chooses to enjoy a pleasure that has no annoying consequences, or one who avoids a pain that produces no resultant pleasure?”

Section 1.10.33 of “de Finibus Bonorum et Malorum”, written by Cicero in 45 BC

“At vero eos et accusamus et iusto odio dignissimos ducimus qui blanditiis praesentium voluptatum deleniti atque corrupti quos dolores et quas molestias excepturi sint occaecati cupiditate non provident, similique sunt in culpa qui officia deserunt mollitia animi, id est laborum et dolorum fuga. Et harum quidem rerum facilis est et expedita distinctio. Nam libero tempore, cum soluta nobis est eligendi optio cumque nihil impedit quo minus id quod maxime placeat facere possimus, omnis voluptas assumenda est, omnis dolor repellendus. Temporibus autem quibusdam et aut officiis debitis aut rerum necessitatibus saepe eveniet ut et voluptates repudiandae sint et molestiae non recusandae. Itaque earum rerum hic tenetur a sapiente delectus, ut aut reiciendis voluptatibus maiores alias consequatur aut perferendis doloribus asperiores repellat.”

Baptism Process

Find some water

A person may be baptized anywhere – a river, pool, bathtub, hot tub, lake or the ocean. The Bible does not limit where someone must be baptized because the power of God’s forgiveness is in Jesus and not the water.

Go into the water

If the water source is large enough (like a pool, river, or baptistry), then you may go into the water with the one being baptized. If it is too small (like a bathtub), then just kneel next to the person as he/she sits down into the water.

Take their confession of faith

Ask, “Do you believe that Jesus is the Son of God? And do you trust Jesus as your Savior and commit to obey Jesus as your Lord?”

What to say

Say, “I now baptize you in the name of the Father, Son and Holy Spirit.” (This is what Jesus taught us to say in Matthew 28:19)

Lay the person down into the water and back up

Full immersion best represents dying to our old selves and being reborn in Jesus, but don’t be alarmed if someone’s elbow or toe pops up during the baptism. Jesus does the saving – not the water. Bonus point: Have the person hold his/her nose.

is the beginning of our faith and our gateway into the Church.","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_weight":"500"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"143f8d38","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

It is the first of three Sacraments of Initiation. Through Baptism we are freed from sin and reborn as sons of God; we become members of Christ, are incorporated into the Church and made sharers in her mission: "Baptism is the sacrament of regeneration through water in the word".

works","align":"center","title_color":"#FFFFFF"},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"}],"isInner":false}],"isInner":false},{"id":"37bce972","elType":"section","settings":{"structure":"30","padding":{"unit":"px","top":"40","right":"0","bottom":"80","left":"0","isLinked":false}},"elements":[{"id":"147a88c5","elType":"column","settings":{"_column_size":33,"_inline_size":60.598},"elements":[{"id":"5c8b9ab9","elType":"widget","settings":{"editor":"

How do I have my child baptized?

“But I must explain to you how all this mistaken idea of denouncing pleasure and praising pain was born and I will give you a complete account of the system, and expound the actual teachings of the great explorer of the truth, the master-builder of human happiness. No one rejects, dislikes, or avoids pleasure itself, because it is pleasure, but because those who do not know how to pursue pleasure rationally encounter consequences that are extremely painful. Nor again is there anyone who loves or pursues or desires to obtain pain of itself, because it is pain, but because occasionally circumstances occur in which toil and pain can procure him some great pleasure. To take a trivial example, which of us ever undertakes laborious physical exercise, except to obtain some advantage from it? But who has any right to find fault with a man who chooses to enjoy a pleasure that has no annoying consequences, or one who avoids a pain that produces no resultant pleasure?”

Section 1.10.33 of “de Finibus Bonorum et Malorum”, written by Cicero in 45 BC

“At vero eos et accusamus et iusto odio dignissimos ducimus qui blanditiis praesentium voluptatum deleniti atque corrupti quos dolores et quas molestias excepturi sint occaecati cupiditate non provident, similique sunt in culpa qui officia deserunt mollitia animi, id est laborum et dolorum fuga. Et harum quidem rerum facilis est et expedita distinctio. Nam libero tempore, cum soluta nobis est eligendi optio cumque nihil impedit quo minus id quod maxime placeat facere possimus, omnis voluptas assumenda est, omnis dolor repellendus. Temporibus autem quibusdam et aut officiis debitis aut rerum necessitatibus saepe eveniet ut et voluptates repudiandae sint et molestiae non recusandae. Itaque earum rerum hic tenetur a sapiente delectus, ut aut reiciendis voluptatibus maiores alias consequatur aut perferendis doloribus asperiores repellat.”

The Bible does not limit where someone must be baptized because the power of God’s forgiveness is in Jesus and not the water.","header_size":"div","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Rubik","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":14,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.5,"sizes":[]},"__globals__":{"typography_typography":""}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"3d42a2a8","elType":"widget","settings":{"text":"Divider","color":"#6F717366","__globals__":{"color":""}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"divider"},{"id":"566cd389","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Go into the water","header_size":"h4","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":14,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"49d130f4","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"If the water source is large enough (like a pool, river, or baptistry), then you may go into the water with the one being baptized. If it is too small (like a bathtub), then just kneel next to the person as he/she sits down into the water.","header_size":"div","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Rubik","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":14,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.5,"sizes":[]},"__globals__":{"typography_typography":""}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"6979d44b","elType":"widget","settings":{"text":"Divider","color":"#6F717366","__globals__":{"color":""}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"divider"},{"id":"591a1b9d","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Take their confession of faith","header_size":"h4","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":14,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"4374a872","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Ask, “Do you believe that Jesus is the Son of God? And do you trust Jesus as your Savior and commit to obey Jesus as your Lord?”","header_size":"div","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Rubik","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":14,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.5,"sizes":[]},"__globals__":{"typography_typography":""}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"22914b43","elType":"widget","settings":{"text":"Divider","color":"#6F717366","__globals__":{"color":""}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"divider"},{"id":"78b86a1b","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"What to say","header_size":"h4","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":14,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"79cf0fd","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Say, “I now baptize you in the name of the Father, Son and Holy Spirit.” (This is what Jesus taught us to say in Matthew 28:19)","header_size":"div","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Rubik","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":14,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.5,"sizes":[]},"__globals__":{"typography_typography":""}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"7fbd09ea","elType":"widget","settings":{"text":"Divider","color":"#6F717366","__globals__":{"color":""}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"divider"},{"id":"3822e6d6","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Lay the person down into the water and back up","header_size":"h4","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":14,"sizes":[]}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"},{"id":"5b103e4e","elType":"widget","settings":{"title":"Full immersion best represents dying to our old selves and being reborn in Jesus, but don’t be alarmed if someone’s elbow or toe pops up during the baptism. Jesus does the saving – not the water. Bonus point: Have the person hold his/her nose.","header_size":"div","typography_typography":"custom","typography_font_family":"Rubik","typography_font_size":{"unit":"px","size":14,"sizes":[]},"typography_font_weight":"400","typography_line_height":{"unit":"em","size":1.5,"sizes":[]},"__globals__":{"typography_typography":""}},"elements":[],"widgetType":"heading"}],"isInner":false}],"isInner":false}]

item.title: Who We Are Export
item.dc:creator: nativechurch
item.content:encoded:
Message from Josephine Williams

We lead people to become disciples of Jesus Christ

Morbi porta malesuada ligula eget efficitur. Etiam non velit nec lacus tempor tempor. Duis pretium, massa non porttitor mollis, ante lacus vestibulum magna, in bibendum odio orci non leo. Aenean aliquam pretium ipsum non bibendum ante lacus vestibulum magna. Duis pretium, massa non porttitor mollis, ante lacus vestibulum magna, in bibendum odio orci non leo. Aenean aliquam pretium ipsum non bibendum ante lacus vestibulum magna.

Aliquam hendrerit porttitor ex vitae feugiat. Integer placerat augue sit amet nibh porttitor blandit. Morbi porta malesuada ligula eget efficitur. Etiam non velit nec lacus tempor tempor. Duis pretium, massa non porttitor mollis, ante lacus vestibulum magna, in bibendum odio orci non leo. Aenean aliquam pretium ipsum non bibendum ante lacus vestibulum magna.

Our Beliefs

The Trinity

Scripture

Salvation

Atonement

Christian Life

The Chapel

Baptism

Marriage

Our Story

Native Chapel membership is a commitment to one another—a willing pledge to guard, care, submit, support, and minister together for the building up of our church body.

Our Values

We value the empowering presence of the Holy Spirit in our midst, marking us as God’s people and manifesting Himself miraculously.

item.title: Home
item.dc:creator: nativechurch
item.content:encoded:
Your Community.
Your Chapel.
Your People.

Check out COVID guidelines before you visit the Chapel. See details

About Chapel

We believe that as we allow the Holy Spirit to reveal His purposes
and to presence Himself among His Church, people’s lives will be transformed.
Our leadership is Spirit-led and our activities and programs are Spirit directed.

Worship

Join on Sundays at 09:15 & 10:45 in the morning and on demand.

Watch Live Streaming

Grow

Connect with others in the community and grow in faith & worship.

Your Community

Connect

Need prayer, counselling, or have questions? We want to help.

Get Help

Belong , Believe  and Transform

This Weekend

The Holy Spirit’s presence assures us of our relationship with Christ. He guides believers into all truth and exalts Christ. He convicts people of their sin, God’s righteousness, and the coming judgment. He comforts us, gives us spiritual gifts, and makes us more like Christ.

Learn More Next Thing 06 April Upcoming Event

Morning of Praise

Music Ministry days hr min sec [eventer_list month_filter="345" series="" count="2"]

Sermons

  • Soul Detox

    Watch Series

  • Jesus - Changes Everything

    Watch Series

  • His & Hers

    Watch Series

  • Pneuma - Life in the spirit

    Watch Series
Native Community Chapel

Podcast

Listen to our podcasts available for everyone including the youth & kids. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Nulla convallis egestas rhoncus.

Subscribe Now

Activities changing lives

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Nulla convallis egestas rhoncus. Donec facilisis fermentum sem, ac viverra ante luctus vel. Donec vel mauris quam.

Start your journey

Join your
community

Start Here

item.title: Contact Us
item.dc:creator: nativechurch
item.content:encoded:
Facebook Twitter Youtube

Contact Us

Membership Process

Morbi porta malesuada ligula eget efficitur. Etiam non velit nec lacus tempor tempor. Duis pretium, massa non porttitor mollis. Start Now

Chapel Address

The Native Chapel
1009 South Acadian Thruway
Baton Rouge, Louisiana 70808

Mailing Address

P.O. Box 4009
Baton Rouge, Louisiana
70821-2028

Phone

Tel: 1800-190-0091
Fax: 1800-190-0092

Kids

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Ut elit tellus, luctus nec ullamcorper mattis, pulvinar dapibus leo.

Apply

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Ut elit tellus, luctus nec ullamcorper mattis, pulvinar dapibus leo. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XHOmBV4js_E

Chapel Education

Social responsibility becomes an aspect not of Christian mission only, but also of Christian conversion. It is impossible to be truly converted to God without being thereby converted to our neighbour.

Learn More

Leadership

Josephine Williams

Founding & Senior Pastor

Jazlyn Grimes

Kids Pastor

Jayden Kuhic

Students Pastor

Sadye Jones

Groups & Discipleship Pastor

item.content:encoded:

Online Giving

Other Ways to give

Mail a gift

P.O. Box 4009
Baton Rouge, Louisiana
70821-2028

item.title: Know Jesus
item.dc:creator: nativechurch
item.content:encoded:
When you learn how to know Jesus, you begin an amazing journey into God's plan for your life.

“You are the light of the world. A city set on a hill cannot be hidden.”
Matthew 5:14

“Arise, shine, for your light has come, and the glory of the Lord has risen upon you.”
Isaiah 60:1

Download our
Free E-Book

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Nulla convallis egestas rhoncus. Donec facilisis fermentum sem, ac viverra ante luctus vel. Donec vel mauris quam.

Do not worry, we won't spam your email inbox.

Jesus Christ is the very representation and likeness of God. He was sent to show us the beauty and majesty of the King. He did not make His debut on that first Christmas morning in Bethlehem because He existed before creation “in the beginning.” In fact, it was “through Him” that “all things were made.” (John 1:3) Even when we look back to the book of Genesis, we read that God said, “Let us make man in our image” (Genesis 1:26) referencing the presence of Christ.

“In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God …. And the Word became flesh, and dwelt among us …. No one has seen God at any time; the only begotten God who is in the bosom of the Father, He has explained Him.”

John 1:1, 14, 18
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oh1Y0Xvs2Zs

How to know Jesus

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kvcVbMcRUkk

Does Jesus Know You?

item.title: Baptism
item.dc:creator: nativechurch
item.content:encoded:
Baptism is the beginning of our faith and our gateway into the Church.

It is the first of three Sacraments of Initiation. Through Baptism we are freed from sin and reborn as sons of God; we become members of Christ, are incorporated into the Church and made sharers in her mission: "Baptism is the sacrament of regeneration through water in the word".

Related Events

[eventer_grid layout="featured" ids="2423" month_filter="345" series="" column="2" count="1"]

How it works

How do I have my child baptized?

“But I must explain to you how all this mistaken idea of denouncing pleasure and praising pain was born and I will give you a complete account of the system, and expound the actual teachings of the great explorer of the truth, the master-builder of human happiness. No one rejects, dislikes, or avoids pleasure itself, because it is pleasure, but because those who do not know how to pursue pleasure rationally encounter consequences that are extremely painful. Nor again is there anyone who loves or pursues or desires to obtain pain of itself, because it is pain, but because occasionally circumstances occur in which toil and pain can procure him some great pleasure. To take a trivial example, which of us ever undertakes laborious physical exercise, except to obtain some advantage from it? But who has any right to find fault with a man who chooses to enjoy a pleasure that has no annoying consequences, or one who avoids a pain that produces no resultant pleasure?”

Section 1.10.33 of “de Finibus Bonorum et Malorum”, written by Cicero in 45 BC

“At vero eos et accusamus et iusto odio dignissimos ducimus qui blanditiis praesentium voluptatum deleniti atque corrupti quos dolores et quas molestias excepturi sint occaecati cupiditate non provident, similique sunt in culpa qui officia deserunt mollitia animi, id est laborum et dolorum fuga. Et harum quidem rerum facilis est et expedita distinctio. Nam libero tempore, cum soluta nobis est eligendi optio cumque nihil impedit quo minus id quod maxime placeat facere possimus, omnis voluptas assumenda est, omnis dolor repellendus. Temporibus autem quibusdam et aut officiis debitis aut rerum necessitatibus saepe eveniet ut et voluptates repudiandae sint et molestiae non recusandae. Itaque earum rerum hic tenetur a sapiente delectus, ut aut reiciendis voluptatibus maiores alias consequatur aut perferendis doloribus asperiores repellat.”

Baptism Process

Find some water

A person may be baptized anywhere – a river, pool, bathtub, hot tub, lake or the ocean. The Bible does not limit where someone must be baptized because the power of God’s forgiveness is in Jesus and not the water.

Go into the water

If the water source is large enough (like a pool, river, or baptistry), then you may go into the water with the one being baptized. If it is too small (like a bathtub), then just kneel next to the person as he/she sits down into the water.

Take their confession of faith

Ask, “Do you believe that Jesus is the Son of God? And do you trust Jesus as your Savior and commit to obey Jesus as your Lord?”

What to say

Say, “I now baptize you in the name of the Father, Son and Holy Spirit.” (This is what Jesus taught us to say in Matthew 28:19)

Lay the person down into the water and back up

Full immersion best represents dying to our old selves and being reborn in Jesus, but don’t be alarmed if someone’s elbow or toe pops up during the baptism. Jesus does the saving – not the water. Bonus point: Have the person hold his/her nose.

item.title: Who we are
item.dc:creator: nativechurch
item.content:encoded:
Message from Josephine Williams

We lead people to become disciples of Jesus Christ

Morbi porta malesuada ligula eget efficitur. Etiam non velit nec lacus tempor tempor. Duis pretium, massa non porttitor mollis, ante lacus vestibulum magna, in bibendum odio orci non leo. Aenean aliquam pretium ipsum non bibendum ante lacus vestibulum magna. Duis pretium, massa non porttitor mollis, ante lacus vestibulum magna, in bibendum odio orci non leo. Aenean aliquam pretium ipsum non bibendum ante lacus vestibulum magna.

Aliquam hendrerit porttitor ex vitae feugiat. Integer placerat augue sit amet nibh porttitor blandit. Morbi porta malesuada ligula eget efficitur. Etiam non velit nec lacus tempor tempor. Duis pretium, massa non porttitor mollis, ante lacus vestibulum magna, in bibendum odio orci non leo. Aenean aliquam pretium ipsum non bibendum ante lacus vestibulum magna.

Our Beliefs

The Trinity

Scripture

Salvation

Atonement

Christian Life

The Chapel

Baptism

Marriage

Our Story

Native Chapel membership is a commitment to one another—a willing pledge to guard, care, submit, support, and minister together for the building up of our church body.

Our Values

We value the empowering presence of the Holy Spirit in our midst, marking us as God’s people and manifesting Himself miraculously.
